items.name: Stinger Helmet MK2 (Black)
items.description:
More Accessories here: https://www.roblox.com/communities/16547842/Akari-Outpost-8#!/store

Introducing the Stinger MK2 Helmet:

The Stinger MK2 Helmet redefines lightweight protection with cutting-edge technology. A marvel of engineering, it streamlines the bulky sensors of its predecessor, the MK1, by housing them internally. This innovative design not only reduces weight but also enhances durability, making it an ideal choice for those who demand agility without compromising on safety. Crafted with advanced materials, the MK2 is not just lighter; it's stronger, offering unparalleled comfort and resilience for those navigating the harshest of environments. Step into the future of headgear with the Stinger MK2, where sleek design meets uncompromising strength.

items.name: ARES GEN-3 Exoskeleton [Torso] [Grey]
items.description:
The third-generation Augmented Reflex Enhancement System (ARES) is a state-of-the-art exoskeleton frame developed by the BLKT Corporation, widely regarded as one of their flagship military assets. Standard-issue across BLKT's military forces, the exoskeleton has also surfaced repeatedly beyond official channels, rumored to have been funneled into black markets amid the ongoing corporate war.

items.name: Soviet Officer Maps Satchel
items.description:
This leather satchel was given out to Soviet officers to store documents and maps and was produced in the 80s-90s.

Matching clothing can be found here:
https://www.roblox.com/groups/34923647/Odema-Clothing-Factory#!/about

"If trouble suddenly breaks out in the Motherland,
A soldier will sound the trumpet
My army — forever you guard the frontier!
You, my heart, and fate above all!

A weight upon destiny for those in the military
Love is harsh but true as true can be
We're all ready for military service
We all! We all have seen our share of battle marches and declare!
We, from the soldier to the marshal, are all one family!"

Special thanks to comrade Kazachenko for creating this item.
